name: Spring Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 cup cooked chicken or pork, shredded
Peanut or vegetable oil for frying
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat. Add garlic and ginger, and saute briefly.
2. Add chicken, cabbage, bean sprouts (if using), carrots, green onions, soy sauce, and season with salt and pepper to taste. Stir-fry over high heat until the vegetables are cooked and all liquid has evaporated, about 3 to 5 minutes.
3. Cool the mixture to room temperature to prevent the wrapper from becoming soggy.
4. In the center of each wonton wrapper, place approximately 1 tablespoon of the filling.
5. Moisten the edges of the wrapper with a little water, fold two opposite edges in toward the center, and roll the wrapper over the filling, starting and ending with the unfolded edges.
6. Moisten the flap to seal it and let the roll dry.
7. In a large Dutch oven, heat 1 inch of oil to 375 degrees. Fry the spring rolls for about 2 minutes on each side until golden brown.
8. Turn the rolls over with tongs and fry another 2 minutes.
9. Remove the rolls from the oil using a slotted spoon or tongs and hold them vertically to allow excess oil to drain.
10. Drain on paper towels.
11. Serve immediately with Dipping Sauce.
Makes 20 to 25 spring rolls
